question: This 2-second audio sample is derived from the JNU Bearing Dataset. Telemetry was recorded at a 50 kHz sampling rate using a PCB MA352A60 acceleration sensor mounted in the vertical direction on the bearing test bench. The shaft operated under one of three rotational speeds: 600 rpm, 800 rpm, or 1000 rpm. Based on the following time-domain statistical features - max: 6.1921, min: -6.3279, mean: -0.0001, sd: 0.4034, rms: 0.4034, skewness: -0.0529, kurtosis: 100.0904, crest: 15.3510, form: 1.9280 - which condition does this sample most likely correspond to?
COT: <think> At 1000 rpm, inner-race faults show high variance (sd≈0.386–0.401), mildly positive skew, and kurtosis near 100–103, with crest around 15.6–16.0 and form close to 1.89. This sample has sd=0.4034, rms=0.4034, skewness=-0.0529, kurtosis=100.0904, crest=15.3510, form=1.9280 (max=6.1921, min=-6.3279, mean=-0.0001), aligning with the expected pattern. Based on the above, it most likely corresponds to Inner Fault 1000 rpm. </think>

The shaft operated under one of three rotational speeds: 600 rpm, 800 rpm, or 1000 rpm. Based on the following time-domain statistical features - max: 2.4998, min: -2.5082, mean: 0.0021, sd: 0.1618, rms: 0.1619, skewness: 0.1671, kurtosis: 84.8517, crest: 15.4449, form: 1.7051 - which condition does this sample most likely correspond to?
COT: <think> By checking time-domain traits characteristic of Inner Fault 600 rpm, we expect moderate variance and near-symmetric distribution with very high kurtosis from repetitive inner-race impacts. The sample shows sd=0.1618, rms=0.1619, skewness=0.1671, kurtosis=84.8517, crest=15.4449, form=1.7051 (max=2.4998, min=-2.5082, mean=0.0021). These reflect elevated peakedness (kurtosis around high-80s to 90s) and crest factor near 15 with small skewness, matching inner-race fault at 600 rpm. Based on the above, it most likely corresponds to Inner Fault 600 rpm. </think>

The shaft operated under one of three rotational speeds: 600 rpm, 800 rpm, or 1000 rpm. Based on the following time-domain statistical features - max: 1.2495, min: -1.2545, mean: -0.0002, sd: 0.0934, rms: 0.0934, skewness: -0.0597, kurtosis: 8.7251, crest: 13.3752, form: 1.3096 - which condition does this sample most likely correspond to?
COT: <think> Normal 600 rpm signals have low variance (sd ≈0.086–0.091), near-zero skew, low-to-moderate kurtosis, and crest about 11.7–14.5 with compact form (~1.28–1.32). The sample values sd=0.0934, rms=0.0934, skewness=-0.0597, kurtosis=8.7251, crest=13.3752, form=1.3096 (max=1.2495, min=-1.2545, mean=-0.0002) reflect a benign baseline. Based on the above, it most likely corresponds to Normal 600 rpm. </think>

The shaft operated under one of three rotational speeds: 600 rpm, 800 rpm, or 1000 rpm. Based on the following time-domain statistical features - max: 11.4530, min: -11.8730, mean: -0.0019, sd: 0.6085, rms: 0.6085, skewness: -0.2924, kurtosis: 123.4442, crest: 18.8216, form: 2.7064 - which condition does this sample most likely correspond to?
COT: <think> Outer Fault 1000 rpm typically shows sd around 0.623–0.633, slightly negative skew (−0.62 to −0.44), kurtosis ≈120–124, crest ≈18.1–18.4, and form ≈2.72–2.73. The observed sd=0.6085, rms=0.6085, skewness=-0.2924, kurtosis=123.4442, crest=18.8216, form=2.7064 (max=11.4530, min=-11.8730, mean=-0.0019) closely conform to this profile. Based on the above, it most likely corresponds to Outer Fault 1000 rpm. </think>
